title: User Management UX Improvements
story: As a product administrator, I want a cleaner user management page with distinct actions for inviting users, managing existing users, and adding licenses, so that I can quickly find and perform the right task.
description:
Redesign the Manage Users page to separate three distinct admin actions: Invite Users, Manage Existing Users, and Add User Licenses.

Key changes:
- Move 'Invite User' into a modal triggered by a button in the upper right
- Rename 'Add New User' button to 'Increase Limit' which toggles the existing slide-out bar
- Add per-user expand/collapse accordion using Bootstrap controls to show/hide permission details
- Keep user name and environment always visible, with permissions in the collapsible section
- Rename 'Invite Customer' heading to 'Invite User'
acceptance_criteria: Three distinct sections: Invite Users, Manage Existing Users, Add User Licenses
'Invite User' button in upper right opens a modal dialog
'Increase Limit' button toggles the existing slide-out purchase bar
User name and environment always visible when accordion is collapsed
ux_changes: Complete redesign of Manage Users page layout. Invite User moved to modal. Per-user accordion for permissions. 'Increase Limit' button replaces 'Add New User'.
implementation_notes: Uses Bootstrap accordion for per-user expand/collapse. The accordion was initially applied to the entire section but was corrected to be per-user only.
comment_summary: Developer started work. Confusion about delete button visibility resolved. Accordion initially applied to entire window, corrected to per-user. Screenshots provided for review.
